questions.question: To convert a decimal to a percentage, you shift the decimal point two places in which direction?
options: It depends on the size of the decimal
questions.explanation: To convert a decimal to a percentage, you multiply by 100, which moves the decimal point two places to the right.

questions.question: Convert the decimal $0.03$ to a percentage.
questions.explanation: Move the decimal point two places to the right: $0.03 \to 3.0 \to 3\%$.

questions.question: Convert $65\%$ to a decimal.
questions.explanation: $65\% = \frac{65}{100} = 0.65$. Shift the decimal point two places to the left.

questions.question: Express $0.0075$ as a percentage.
questions.explanation: Shift the decimal point two places to the right: $0.0075 \to 0.75\%$.

questions.question: Convert $150\%$ into a decimal number.
questions.explanation: $150\% = \frac{150}{100} = 1.5$. Shift the decimal point two places to the left.
